source: ISO 11161:2007 Safety of Machinery + OSHA 29 CFR 1910.212 + ANSI B11 Series Machine Safety Standards
topic: Safe operating zones, clearances, and exclusion areas around industrial machinery

facts.context: OSHA 1910.212 — 36 in minimum clear zone in front of any machine where operator stands
Minimum rear clearance for maintenance access — 30 in ANSI B11 general guideline
Minimum side clearance between machines where no operator access is needed — 24 in
CNC machining centre — operator standing zone depth including door swing and chip clearance
Press brake — minimum front clear zone for sheet metal handling and operator safety
OSHA 1910.217 — 30 in clear on each side of powered conveyor for maintenance walkway
ISO 10218-2 — safety fence or light curtain must be at least 1.8m from robot reach envelope
Minimum distance between robot reach envelope and safety fence inner face
NFPA 51B — 10 ft radius exclusion zone around welding operations for combustibles
Minimum clear zone for welder to maneuver with equipment and PPE
Minimum radial clear zone around lathe for chip ejection — 48 in from chuck centreline
ANSI B11.9 — 60 in exclusion arc in front of grinding wheel for wheel burst protection
General industrial standard — minimum gap between machine footprints for operator and maintenance access
NEC 110.26 — 36 in minimum clear in front of electrical panel — must never be blocked by equipment
Minimum clear access zone around compressed air distribution manifolds and valves
Coolant filling and filtration station — minimum operator access clearance
QC inspection station — depth of clear zone for inspector with parts and measuring equipment
QC station — minimum width for single inspector workstation
Minimum clear zone in front of storage rack face for manual picking access
ISO 13851 — E-stop must be reachable within 600mm from any operator position at the machine
